new_classes.label_en: Wine with Protected Geographical Indication (PGI) in containers ‚â§ 2 L (subheading 2204 21 80)
new_classes.label_de: Wein mit gesch√ºtzter geografischer Angabe (g.g.A.) in Beh√§ltnissen mit einem Inhalt von 2 l oder weniger (Unterposition 2204 21 80)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes bearing a Protected Geographical Indication (PGI) as defined in additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22, presented in containers of a nominal volume not exceeding 2 litres, classified under CN subheading 2204 21 80.

A quality borne by a wine product indicating that it holds a Protected Geographical Indication (PGI) as established by EU wine legislation and referenced in additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22 of the CN, distinct from a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO).

coverage_explanation: The two primary distinguishing criteria from the legal text are covered: (1) the wine bears a Protected Geographical Indication (PGI) ‚Äî captured by the new `ProtectedGeographicalIndicationQuality` class and the `someValuesFrom` restriction on `RO:has_quality`; (2) the container volume is ‚â§ 2 L ‚Äî captured by the `someValuesFrom` restriction on `BFO:has_part` pointing to the existing `eucn:SmallContainer` class. Sibling disjointness is asserted against the co-listed PDO wines (22042168, 22042174), Tokaj (22042123), and other wines (22042178) in the 2204 21 family. A minor gap remains: additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22 contains detailed criteria defining PGI wines (specific EU wine regulation references, geographic link requirements) which cannot be fully formalised in OWL-DL without importing the underlying wine regulation ontology. The `ProtectedGeographicalIndicationQuality` is introduced as a new class because the TBox already contains `ProtectedDesignationOfOriginOrGeographicalIndicationQuality` (a combined PDO/PGI quality) but no PGI-specific quality; the complement restriction on that broader quality class provides further disambiguation. The `complement` restrictions using `owl:equivalentClass` as property_iri are placeholder disjointness assertions and may need to be expressed as `owl:disjointWith` axioms in the serialiser; the semantics of mutual exclusion with siblings are captured at the class level via the PGI-only quality constraint.
